content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Review found two lifecycle issues: reopened same-run resumes preserve the old attempt timestamp, which breaks failed-resume milestone sync, and the public persist_plan path leaves finalized milestones in planning without emitting the required ready transition.

## Amendments

1. Fix resumed same-run attempts so the lineage row is retargeted to the new start time. In src/adapters/fs.rs:3510-3554, FsTaskRunLineageStore::reopen_failed_exact_attempt clones the previously failed row but never updates started_at, so a fail -> resume (same run_id) sequence keeps the original attempt timestamp. That stale timestamp means the resumed run no longer matches the exact-lineage checks in src/cli/run.rs:489-520 and src/cli/run.rs:721-734; if the resumed run then fails, sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options bails out at src/cli/run.rs:228-244 and skips milestone terminal sync. Update reopened entries to use the new started_at and add a regression test that covers fail -> resume same run_id -> fail again.
   Rationale: This is a real correctness bug: a durably resumed attempt can still be treated as missing lineage, leaving the milestone stuck in the pre-failure state after a failed resume.
2. Make the direct plan-finalization API advance the milestone to ready and emit the planning -> ready lifecycle event. The public persist_plan / persist_plan_locked path in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1485-1507 and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1567-1711 writes the exported plan artifacts and updates plan_hash/plan_version, but it leaves the snapshot in planning. Only materialize_bundle adds the ready transition afterward in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1400-1409. Any caller that uses persist_plan directly ends up with a finalized, exported plan that still reports planning and has no StatusChanged event for planning -> ready. Either fold the ready transition into plan finalization or make this impossible to misuse, and add coverage for the direct persist_plan entry point.
   Rationale: This violates the lifecycle contract for the public service API and creates a footgun where callers can silently miss the required ready state and journal event.
